original_question:
Triangle $\triangle ABC$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $AB = 6, AC = 4,$ and $\angle BAC = 90^\circ.$ Let $D$ be the reflection across $\overline{BC}$ of the centroid of $\triangle ABC. {}$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $1, 2, 3,$ and $r$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $A, B, C,$ and $D,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $r$ can be written as $\tfrac mn,$ where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m+n.$

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Triangle \triangle XYZ lies in plane \mathcal P with XY = 8, XZ = 6, and \angle XZY = 90^\circ. Let W be the reflection across \overline{YZ} of the centroid of \triangle XYZ. Four spheres, all on the same side of \mathcal P, have radii 1, 3, 4, and s and are tangent to \mathcal P at points X, Y, Z, and W, respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane \mathcal T and are all on the same side of \mathcal T. The value of s can be written as \tfrac {p}{q}, where p and q are relatively prime positive integers. Find p+q.

Triangle \triangle DEF lies in plane \mathcal P with DE = 10, DF = 5, and \angle EDF = 90^\circ. Let G be the reflection across \overline{EF} of the centroid of \triangle DEF. Four spheres, all on the same side of \mathcal P, have radii 2, 4, 5, and t and are tangent to \mathcal P at points D, E, F, and G, respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane \mathcal T and are all on the same side of \mathcal T. The value of t can be written as \tfrac {u}{v}, where u and v are relatively prime positive integers. Find u+v.

Triangle \triangle PQR lies in plane \mathcal P with PQ = 7, PR = 3, and \angle PQR = 90^\circ. Let S be the reflection across \overline{QR} of the centroid of \triangle PQR. Four spheres, all on the same side of \mathcal P, have radii 1, 2, 5, and k and are tangent to \mathcal P at points P, Q, R, and S, respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane \mathcal T and are all on the same side of \mathcal T. The value of k can be written as \tfrac {x}{y}, where x and y are relatively prime positive integers. Find x+y.

Triangle \triangle JKL lies in plane \mathcal P with JK = 9, JL = 4, and \angle JKL = 90^\circ. Let M be the reflection across \overline{KL} of the centroid of \triangle JKL. Four spheres, all on the same side of \mathcal P, have radii 1, 2, 6, and z and are tangent to \mathcal P at points J, K, L, and M, respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane \mathcal T and are all on the same side of \mathcal T. The value of z can be written as \tfrac {a}{b}, where a and b are relatively prime positive integers. Find a+b.

Triangle \triangle RST lies in plane \mathcal P with RS = 5, RT = 12, and \angle RST = 90^\circ. Let N be the reflection across \overline{ST} of the centroid of \triangle RST. Four spheres, all on the same side of \mathcal P, have radii 2, 3, 5, and q and are tangent to \mathcal P at points R, S, T, and N, respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane \mathcal T and are all on the same side of \mathcal T. The value of q can be written as \tfrac {c}{d}, where c and d are relatively prime positive integers. Find c+d.
